source.prompt:
Project title: What.
Story mode: short_story; target platform: local_only.
Section 1 of 3: The Late Night Incident.
Section purpose: Introduce the eerie circumstances surrounding Carlo's experience..
Beat 1 of 9 in this section.
Previous beat narration: none; this opens the section.
Current beat narration: At 11:46 PM, during a brownout in my grandmother’s house, my cousin Carlo sat straight up in bed and whispered:
Next beat narration: “Mom… there’s someone underneath.”

Visual target:
A dim candle flickering in a dark bedroom, shadows creeping across the walls.

Shot type: establishing
Camera distance: medium
Lighting: dim candlelight
Lens: 50mm
Composition: centered subject
Subject continuity: Carlo's bedroom remains consistent
Avoid (beat-specific): no bright colors.

Style preset: fantastical
Look and feel: eerie yet soft
Character anchors: Carlo; his mother; grandmother
Continuity rules: Carlo appears as a child throughout; settings remain consistent during transitions; muddy visuals persist across scenes
Avoid: no photorealism, avoid cluttered backgrounds

Visual direction:
Project feel: eerie animated suspense with emotional intensity
Project pacing: measured slow-burn with sharp turns
Project visual style: stylized animated cinematic frames, explicitly non-photorealistic and non-ghibli style but still soft and fantastical
Section feel: intense and suspenseful
Section pacing: measured and tense
Section visual style: dark and shadowed with candle light
Style preset: fantastical
Look and feel: eerie yet soft
Palette: dark blue; muddy brown; dim candlelight; soft shadow
Era and location: contemporary, rural environment
Character anchors: Carlo; his mother; grandmother
Continuity rules: Carlo appears as a child throughout; settings remain consistent during transitions; muddy visuals persist across scenes
Avoid: no photorealism, avoid cluttered backgrounds

Create a vertical 9:16 image for the current beat.
Follow the visual direction exactly; do not add a visual medium, rendering style, camera format, or realism level that conflicts with it.
Depict the exact current beat, not a generic mood board and not a later event.
Preserve continuity with the immediately previous and next beats, but do not introduce objects, characters, or reveals that have not happened yet.
Keep anatomy, geometry, lighting, and composition coherent for a vertical frame.
Avoid fake text, UI, subtitles, watermarks, logos, split screens, soundwave graphics, continuity errors, distorted hands or faces, and unintended extra objects or characters.
